 2025 July 31 
 Supernova 2025rbs in NGC 7331 Image Credit: Ben Godson (University of Warwick) 
 Explanation:  A long time ago in a galaxy 50 million light-years away, 
a star exploded. Light from that 
supernova was first detected by telescopes on planet Earth on July 14th though, and the extragalactic transient is now known to astronomers as 
supernova 2025rbs.
